title: People-Centric AI & Robotics
subtitle: Designing embodied intelligent systems that prioritize human needs, safety, and collective societal value
intro: People-Centric AI & Robotics moves beyond optimizing technology or individual user experience toward innovation that serves communities and society. It keeps humans at the center of decision-making across the full lifecycle of a system — design, deployment, and governance — ensuring transparency, trust, and meaningful collaboration between people and machines.

vision.text: Create AI and robotic systems that enhance human capabilities while preserving autonomy, dignity, and social well-being — shifting the focus from individual users to communities, diverse stakeholders, and long-term societal outcomes.

mission.text: Advance human-robot collaboration, social intelligence, and responsible AI innovation through participatory, co-creative methods that deliver real-world impact aligned with human and societal values.

activities.title: Social Robotics
activities.text: Developing socially intelligent robots that interact naturally with diverse users in real-world settings, combining human-centred design, adaptive behaviour, and transparent communication to build trust and support meaningful collaboration.

activities.title: Human–Robot Interaction in the Wild
activities.text: Investigating how people interact with robots in everyday environments through real-world deployments and longitudinal studies. Developing robots that adapt to diverse users, contexts, and social norms while enabling natural and meaningful interactions.

activities.title: Responsible AI & Innovation
activities.text: Ensuring the ethical and safe deployment of Physical AI by conducting participatory design workshops integrating societal values into the development and evaluation of robotic systems, in alignment with the UN Sustainable Development Goals.

publications.title: Robots for Well-being: Design and Integration of a Low-Cost Social Robot Prototype for Promoting Healthy Habits
publications.venue: IEEE International Conference on Advanced Robotics and its Social Impacts (ARSO)

publications.title: From Large Language Models to Agentic AI in Industry 5.0 and the Post-ChatGPT Era: A Socio-Technical Framework and Review on Human–Robot Collaboration

cta.title: Collaborate With Us
cta.text: We are looking for students, researchers, and industry partners interested in people-centered robotics and AI systems.
